g.class: el ma

rect.title:
The Acknowledgement document is a simple notification from party to party acknowledging the receipt of another transaction such as an inventory release or an updated order
It may also provide information indicating the status of the document.
This document is used consistently as an optional response to a notification transaction.

g.class: se co
el op hd

rect.title: An instance of a distribution channel. That is, the set of organizations involved in the development of the current transaction.

g.class: se hd co
el ma hd hd1

rect.title: An organization playing a participating role in an instance of a distribution channel.

g.class: se hd hd1 co
el op hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: The electronic or geographic address which a party has provided as the contact channel. For example, contact email, contact postal address, contact phone number.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 co
el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Uniquely Identifies a set of Contact Information, within the context of one message.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: to identify the contact purpose (PADIS code list 3299)

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: When TRUE, Contact Information was refused to be provided.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: The email address which should be used for contact purposes.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: PADIS code list: Contact Type (to identify the technical contact type information)

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: The email address which should be used for contact purposes.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: A single human being as distinct from a group, class, or family.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: An additional name type. The structure consists of a generic proper name with associated type. Examples: Name - 'Smith'. Name Type Code - 'Fathers Surname'. Name - 'Park'. Name Type Code - 'Mothers Surname'.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 co

line.class: hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5
hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5

rect.title: A proper name defined by the associated name type code and further clarified by the description text. Name - 'Smith'. Name Type Code - 'Father Surname'. Name - 'Park'. Name Type Code - 'Mother Surname'.

line.class: hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5

rect.title: The type of proper name Examples: 'Father Surname'. 'Mother Surname'.

ns0:e.ns0:an: This is the individuals fathers name surname which is part of the individuals full surname.
This is the individuals mothers name surname which is part of the individuals full surname.

line.class: hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4 hd5

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: The date on which an individual was born.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Country Code where the Passenger was born (ISO 3166). For example: DE

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Gender of the individual. Refer to PADIS Codeset 6353.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: A personal name given to the individual at birth and used before a family name. Also called first name. E.g. JOHN.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Uniquely Identifies an Individual within the context of one message.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Individual's middle name or initial. E.g. G, GEORGE.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Individual's name suffixes and letters. Examples: Jr., Sr., III, Ret., Esq.

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Individual's hereditary name(s) common to all members of a family. Also known as family name or last name. E.g. SMITH. Excludes any potential suffix.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: A word such as Mr., Mrs., Miss or Dr that is used before an individual's name to indicate the gender, profession or marital status.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Reference to Individual instance(s)

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Other Contact Method information. E.g. web site URL, social media handle.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: PADIS code list: Contact Type (to identify the technical contact type information)

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Other Contact Method Value. Example: www.marketingsite.com

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Uniquely identifies a Passenger Segment within the context of one message.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: A telephone number is a sequence of digits assigned to a fixed-line telephone subscriber station connected to a telephone line or to a wireless electronic telephony device, such as a radio telephone or a mobile telephone, or to other devices for data transmission via the public switched telephone network (PSTN) or other private networks.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Phone number area code. Example: 813 Note: The phone number can be put unstructured into the phone element or can be put structured into country dialing code, area code, phone number and extension.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: PADIS code list: Contact Type (to identify the technical contact type information)

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Numeric telephone dialing prefixes for the member countries of the International Telecommunication Union (ITU). Note: The phone number can be put unstructured into the phone element or can be put structured into country dialing code, area code, phone number and extension.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Phone extension number. Example: 1234 Note: The phone number can be put unstructured into the phone element or can be put structured into country dialing code, area code, phone number and extension.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Phone number text. Examples: +1 999-999-9999 ext 1234, 617-9976 Note: This may be a simple, un-structured phone number, such as +01 999-999-9999 ext 1234 or combined with additional attributes country dialing code, area code and extension to create a structured phone number.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Natural or physical address used for postal service. May be a building address, airport address, etc.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 hd3 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Additional address information including building name, room, apartment, or suite number.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: The name, expressed as text, of the city, town or village.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: PADIS code list: Contact Type (to identify the technical contact type information)

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: ISO Country Code (can be extended by bilateral agreement in case of exceptions, e.g. Kosovo). Examples: US, FR.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: The name, expressed as text, of the country.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: The name, expressed as text, of the sub-division of a country.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Post office box. Example: P.O. Box 21

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Postal code. Examples: 11001, 11001-2345

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3 hd4

rect.title: Street name and number. Examples: Main Street, 100 Main Street

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Relationship to the passenger e.g. used for when this individual is an emergency contact

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: The number defining the position of the distribution chain link in the distribution chain indicating the order in which the organization participated.

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: The distribution chain role performed by the participating organization. That is, the Seller (end buyer facing), the Distributor (an intermediary), or the Carrier (the Responsible Supplier). For clarity this is different to the business model used by the participating organization, and the Nature of Business typically registered during incorporation as one of the organization's business activities.

ns0:e.ns0:an: An organization which carries the passenger, baggage, or goods, and/or commits to delivering the carriage.
An organization that provides a distribution capability such as a certain type of Consolidator, an Aggregator, more generally an intermediary.
An organization that offers a shopping capability to a shopper.

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: An organized structure set up for a particular purpose, such as a business, government body, department, charity, or financial institution.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 co
el op hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Name of the organization

g.class: el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: Organization code or identifier.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: An individual conducting sales activities on behalf of a sales branch or organization.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 co
el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: The unique identifier of the sales agent. This value must be accompanied by the appropriate Sales Branch Identifier or Organization Identifier to ensure uniqueness.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: An organizational unit with responsibility over sales in a market, for a product or service and under any other specified criteria.

g.class: se hd hd1 hd2 co
el ma hd hd1 hd2 hd3

rect.title: The industry wide unique identifier of the sales branch. Commonly, a value from the Pseudo City Code may be used, or a bi-latterly agreed code. When the latter it must only be used between parties that hold an appropriate translation.

g.class: el op hd hd1 hd2

rect.title: The root mode of the IATA Verifiable Presentation schema structure based on w3c Verifiable Credential Schema

g.class: el op hd

rect.title: Processing status information.

g.class: se hd co
el ma hd hd1

rect.title: Status Code. Example: OK

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: Additional status message information.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: The code corresponding to the processing status if not OK. e.g FAILED.

g.class: el op hd

rect.title: IATA standard payload attributes.

g.class: se hd co
el op hd hd1

rect.title: Identifies the alternate language for a customer or message. The human language is identified by ISO 639 codes.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: Allow end-to-end correlation of log messages with the corresponding Web service message throughout the processing of the Web service message.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: Identifes the primary language preference for the message. The human language is identified by ISO 639 codes.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: When true, indicates the message is being re-sent after a failure. It is recommended that this attribute is used (i.e., set to true) only when a message is retransmitted.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: Used to identify the sequence number of the transaction as assigned by the sending system; allows for an application to process messages in a certain order or to request a resynchronization of messages in the event that a system has been off-line and needs to retrieve messages that were missed.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: Indicates the creation date and time of the message in UTC.

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: A unique identifier to relate all messages within a transaction (e.g. this would be sent in all request and response messages that are part of an on-going transaction).

g.class: el op hd hd1

rect.title: For all IATA versioned messages, the version of the message is indicated by a decimal value.
